channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How I Ditched Vendor Lock-In — A Practical Guide for 2026
item.description: Look, how I Ditched Vendor Lock-In — A Practical Guide for 2026 I still remember the day I opened my monthly cloud bill and nearly dropped my coffee. Twelve hundred dollars. For what? Running...

item.description: Originally published at kalyna.pro Function calling — what Anthropic calls tool use — lets Claude call code you write: query a database, hit an internal API, run a calculation, or check today's...

item.title: How I Built a Python Script to Export and Analyze Your DEV.to Followers
item.description: It was a Friday, and school was out for the weekend. I made a bit of a Spelling Bee page for my website while I was at school, but that wasn't enough. I was bored and had no clue what to make...

item.title: Linked List Cycle II
item.description: leetcode.com Problem Statement Given the head of a linked list, return the node where the cycle begins. If there is no cycle, return null. You are not allowed to modify the linked list. Example...

item.title: Palindrome Linked List
item.description: leetcode.com Problem Statement Given the head of a singly linked list, return true if the linked list is a palindrome and false otherwise. Example 1 Input: 1 -> 2 -> 2 -> 1 Output: true Example 2...

item.title: Actuando sobre la Seguridad Perimetral: Cómo Implementar un Cortafuegos Dinámico basado en el Comportamiento DNS
item.description: En el panorama actual de la ciberseguridad, los métodos tradicionales de filtrado a menudo se quedan cortos ante usuarios que utilizan VPNs, túneles o servidores DNS externos para evadir políticas...

item.title: Why I don’t feel the excitement when coding now, unlike before because of AI?
item.description: A little ironic that the header image was generated using AI, but oh well... Before, when I create a game, I would go to countless YouTube tutorials. Then getting yelled on countless forums...

item.title: This portable light is great for way more than camping
item.description: It's intended for camping, but BougeRV's T1 light is so versatile that it also makes for a great summer travel companion that'll continue to light the dark corners of your life long after you...

item.title: System Design - 17. Service Discovery & Service Mesh: How Thousands of Services Find Each Other
item.description: Covers: Client-Side vs Server-Side Discovery, Service Registries, Service Mesh (Istio/Envoy), Kubernetes DNS The Problem That Didn't Exist in the Monolith In a monolith, calling another module is...

item.description: Introduction Real-world mobile game power consumption varies significantly across rendering complexity, frame rate, and device workload distribution. Based on collected production data from...

item.title: Bernie Sanders’ AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Plan
item.description: Let no one accuse Bernie Sanders of ducking the big questions. Writing in the New York Times last week, the senator asked: “Will the future of humanity be determined by a handful of billionaires...

item.title: Rethinking MDR as Attackers and Defenders Embrace AI
item.description: For most of the past decade, managed detection and response was the answer to a real problem. Security teams couldn't staff around the clock, couldn't hire enough analysts, and needed someone else...

item.title: You do your own time
item.description: There we were, a regular murderers’ row of librarians. Little Jo. Eustace. And me. Turning around in the nave of our library to greet the sound of footsteps, pistols leveled in case whoever was...

item.title: LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of three now-patched security flaws impacting LangGraph, including a critical vulnerability chain that could result in remote code execution....

item.title: You Probably Won’t Get Rich Off the SpaceX IPO
item.description: The company has set aside an unusually high number of shares for retail investors. Still, experts say, you’re just getting the crumbs.

item.title: Why “reprogramming” is the buzziest approach to reversing aging right now
item.description: Earlier this week, Life Biosciences, a biotech company focused on reversing age-related diseases, announced that it had dosed its first volunteer. A person with glaucoma has had an experimental...

item.title: Inside Interoception: The hidden sense of how you feel inside
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of science and technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. Your...

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
item.description: An INTERPOL-led operation last month resulted in the disruption of Sniper Dz, a decade-long phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) platform, Group-IB said Thursday. The effort, codenamed Operation Ramz,...

item.title: Siri won’t be your AI girlfriend
item.description: Our early testing has already shown that Siri AI knows when to shut up, and that's very much by design. In an interview with Mostly Human spotted by MacRumors, Craig Federighi said Apple's new...

item.title: Europol Disrupts AudiA6 Crypto Laundering Service Used by Ransomware Gangs
item.description: Authorities in Europe have disrupted AudiA6, a cryptocurrency laundering service used by ransomware gangs and cybercriminal networks. Europol, in a statement issued Thursday, said the dismantling...

item.title: Cheaper, faster, and culturally aware, Avataar’s video AI is built for India’s scale
item.description: Avataar AI's distilled video model is priced at $0.005 for every second of generation

item.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
item.description: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.

item.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
item.description: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

item.title: Apple’s Camera Chief Thinks AI Can Give You Superpowers
item.description: The generative features in iOS 27’s new Photos app will add fake pixels to some of your shots, but Apple’s Jon McCormack says the company isn’t using AI “for the sake of AI.”

item.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
item.description: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.

item.title: Why You Might Already Own SpaceX Shares, Siri’s AI Makeover, and Knicks Owner’s Surveillance Machine
item.description: Today on Uncanny Valley, we take an early look at the SpaceX IPO and why you might find yourself among the investors without even realizing it.

item.title: Amazon’s Echo Hub gets a customizable new look and Ring’s AI features
item.description: Amazon's rolling out a free software update for Echo Hub devices that gives the homescreen a much-needed update to the interface it launched with in 2024. It had already added Alex Plus AI...

item.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities...

item.title: Logitech’s awesome MX Master 3S mouse drops to under $100
item.description: The platform-agnostic Logitech MX Master 3S wireless mouse is discounted to $89.99 at Amazon ($30 off), matching the best price we’ve seen so far this year. While it may look like a somewhat...
